skill.name: one-drive-automation
skill.description: Guide agents through OneDrive file search, browsing, uploads, downloads, sharing, permissions, folders, and drive metadata using Rube MCP tools.
skill.summary: Automate common OneDrive workflows through Rube MCP tool sequences.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-cryptography, and network-reconnaissance findings are false positives from Markdown inline code, sample identifiers, OAuth wording, and API terms in SKILL.md. The confirmed risk is legitimate but sensitive OneDrive automation through an external Rube MCP endpoint, including file access, downloads, deletes, sharing links, and permission changes.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Endpoint With OneDrive OAuth Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add an external Rube MCP endpoint and connect OneDrive through Microsoft OAuth. This is expected functionality, but it grants a third-party tool path access to cloud files and metadata.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly names the external MCP URL and OneDrive connection workflow. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but it creates real data-access risk if users do not trust the MCP provider.

medium_findings.title: Permission And Sharing Changes Can Expose Files
medium_findings.description: The skill documents workflows for inviting users, creating share links, and changing roles. Incorrect item IDs or broad write permissions could expose or modify sensitive OneDrive content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The permission-management workflow is clearly documented, including read and write roles plus share links. The skill also warns that wrong item IDs can affect unintended files, confirming the risk context.

medium_findings.title: Mutating File Operations Include Delete, Move, Copy, Upload, And Download
medium_findings.description: The skill guides agents through remote file mutation and transfer operations. These actions are useful, but they can alter, disclose, or remove business files if run without confirmation and ID verification.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented workflows include upload, download, move, copy, and delete tools with concrete parameters. No malicious intent is present, but the operations are inherently sensitive.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The many Ruby or shell backtick findings map to Markdown inline code spans around tool names and parameters, not executable shell or Ruby code. No evidence of shell command execution was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is Markdown guidance and contains no script blocks or executable Ruby. The flagged backticks surround documentation tokens such as tool names and parameter names.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography hits align with ordinary OneDrive, OAuth, ID, URL, or API documentation text. No evidence of cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill describes OAuth and sample item IDs rather than implementing crypto. There are no code paths that hash, encrypt, decrypt, or validate signatures.

low_findings.title: Static Network Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The network-reconnaissance hits are documentation text about requiring sign-in and monitoring asynchronous copy progress. No evidence of port scanning, host discovery, or probing behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines describe OneDrive permission and copy-operation semantics, not reconnaissance. SKILL.md contains no executable code that could scan networks.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Service Connection For Cloud File Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on Rube MCP and OneDrive OAuth. Users should review the provider and connection scopes before granting access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dependency and connection flow are explicit prerequisites. This is not malicious, but it is the central trust boundary for the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Share Link And Permission Mutation Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill supports creating links and granting user access. These operations should require explicit confirmation and least-privilege role selection.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented sequence includes permission inspection, invitation, role selection, and link creation. The risk follows directly from the intended feature.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Delete Operation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a delete workflow that moves items to the recycle bin. Users should verify item IDs and use ETags when available.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The delete tool and concurrency parameter are documented directly. The file states deletion is not permanent, which lowers but does not remove the operational risk.

content.value_statement: Managing OneDrive files across search, sharing, and folders can be slow and error-prone. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured Rube MCP workflows for common OneDrive operations.
actual_capabilities: Search OneDrive files and browse drive or folder contents with current Rube MCP schemas.
Upload files to selected folders and download files by validated OneDrive item IDs.
Create folders, move items, copy items, rename metadata, and delete items to the recycle bin.
Inspect permissions, invite users, assign read or write roles, and create sharing links.
Check drive details, quota information, version history, and supported SharePoint delta workflows.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active OneDrive connection before any workflow can run.
Search does not support KQL operators, wildcard searches, or path-based filtering.
Some operations depend on exact item IDs, folder IDs, drive IDs, and Microsoft Graph behavior.
Copy operations can be asynchronous, and large folders may need pagination and rate-limit handling.

use_cases.title: Find Project Documents Quickly
use_cases.description: Search OneDrive by plain keywords, inspect metadata, and browse folders without manually clicking through large drives.

use_cases.title: Publish And Share Team Files
use_cases.description: Upload files to target folders, verify permissions, invite specific users, and create controlled sharing links.

use_cases.title: Maintain Drive Structure
use_cases.description: Create folders, move files, copy content, review quota, and track supported SharePoint drive changes.

prompt_templates.title: Search For A File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the OneDrive automation skill to find files matching these keywords: [keywords]. Search tool schemas first, then list the most relevant results with names, locations, and web links.

prompt_templates.title: Upload To A Folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the OneDrive automation skill to upload [local file or prepared upload object] into [folder path]. Resolve the folder first, confirm the target, then report the final file name and location.

prompt_templates.title: Share With Specific Users
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the OneDrive automation skill to share [file or folder name] with [recipients]. Check current permissions first, request confirmation for write access, and require sign-in when possible.

prompt_templates.title: Audit And Reorganize A Folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the OneDrive automation skill to review [folder path], identify stale or misplaced files, propose moves or copies, and wait for confirmation before changing or deleting anything.

output_examples.input: Find budget files related to Q4 planning.
output_examples.output: A ranked list of matching OneDrive items with file names, parent folders, modified dates, sizes, and direct web links.

output_examples.input: Share the onboarding folder with a new contractor as read-only.
output_examples.output: A permission summary showing the resolved folder, existing access, the invited recipient, read-only role, sign-in requirement, and any invitation status.

output_examples.input: Clean up duplicate reports in the finance archive.
output_examples.output: A proposed action plan that lists duplicate candidates, destination folders, risk notes, and confirmation steps before any move or delete action.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first so parameters match the current OneDrive schema.
Resolve and verify item IDs, folder IDs, and drive IDs before mutating files or permissions.
Ask for explicit confirmation before delete, write-share, cross-drive copy, or bulk update actions.
anti_patterns: Do not use wildcard, KQL, or path syntax in OneDrive search queries.
Do not create anonymous or broad sharing links without a clear business reason.
Do not run bulk moves or deletes from search results without reviewing exact item IDs.

faq.question: Does this skill require a OneDrive login?
faq.answer: Yes. Rube MCP must be connected, and OneDrive must show an active Microsoft OAuth connection.

faq.question: Can it search inside folders recursively?
faq.answer: It can search by keywords and can inspect folder children, but deep traversal may require pagination and repeated item lookups.

faq.question: Can it overwrite existing files during upload?
faq.answer: The documented upload behavior automatically renames conflicts by default. Review current tool schemas for overwrite options.

faq.question: Can it permanently delete files?
faq.answer: The documented delete workflow moves items to the recycle bin, not permanent deletion.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive documents?
faq.answer: Use it only with trusted Rube MCP access, least-privilege sharing, and confirmation for downloads, deletes, and permission changes.

faq.question: Why does it search tools before each workflow?
faq.answer: Rube MCP schemas can change, so the skill instructs agents to fetch current tool definitions before calling OneDrive tools.
